text.title: swift-format + SwiftLint + Swift Testing Interop
text.summary: Pair Harmonize with swift-format (layout), SwiftLint (idiom), and Swift Testing + XCTest interop (ST-0021) so the deterministic floor is one `swift test` away.

section.title: Single-command floor for agents
section.p: Apple ships `swift-format` in the Xcode 16+ toolchain. SwiftLint adds idiomatic rules. Swift Testing (`@Test`, `#expect`) is the new framework; ST-0021 (accepted March 2026) lets `XCTest`-based suites and `@Test` suites coexist in one process so migrations don't require a big-bang rewrite.

rule.statement: Every Swift project's CI MUST run, in order: `swift-format lint --strict --recursive Sources Tests`, `swiftlint --strict`, `swift test` (which executes Harmonize + unit + integration tests via the ST-0021 bridge). All four steps MUST be green for merge. Agents are forbidden from passing `--quiet` or removing `--strict` to silence findings.
rule.rationale: A single command shape (`swift test`) for both unit and architecture tests means the agent's loop has one signal to interpret; mixing `xcodebuild` and `swift test` introduces format differences that LLMs misparse.

example.code: # bin/check (run by pre-commit and CI) set -euo pipefail swift-format lint --strict --recursive Sources Tests swiftlint --strict swift test --enable-code-coverage
example.note: Same script local + CI; agent reproduces failures verbatim.

example.code: # CI pipeline that drops strictness to "unblock" an agent PR swift-format lint --recursive Sources Tests || true swiftlint || true swift test || true
example.note: Suppressing exit codes turns the floor into theatre.

antipattern.description: Migrating tests to Swift Testing piecemeal but disabling the XCTest target instead of using the ST-0021 interop, leaving entire legacy suites unrun.
antipattern.reason: The interop is what makes incremental migration safe; turning off XCTest leaves a coverage gap exactly while the rewrite is in progress.

section.reference: swift-format config used by the floor.
